extra.message: User input from handler.params['id'] is concatenated directly into SQL string without parameterization. The 'id' parameter is injected directly into the SQL query allowing attackers to modify the query logic.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

extra.message: User input from handler.params['username'] and params['password'] are concatenated directly into SQL string without parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL to bypass authentication or exfiltrate data.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

extra.message: User-supplied 'msg' parameter from handler.params is rendered directly into HTML response without escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the 'msg' query parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Stored comments are retrieved from database and rendered into HTML without escaping. User-supplied comment data is placed directly in <td> tags allowing stored XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from handler.params['domain'] is passed to subprocess.check_output with shell=True. An attacker can inject shell metacharacters to execute arbitrary commands on the system.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: User-supplied 'path' parameter is passed directly to os.path.abspath() and open() without validation. An attacker can use directory traversal sequences (../) to read arbitrary files on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from handler.params['object'] is decoded from base64 and passed directly to pickle.loads(). Pickle is unsafe and can execute arbitrary code during deserialization, allowing Remote Code Execution.
User input from handler.params['path'] is injected directly into window.location.replace() without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs for phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: Attacker-supplied session ID from handler.params['session'] is accepted and set as the SESSIONID cookie without validation. This allows session fixation attacks where an attacker can force a user to use a known session ID.

User-supplied path from self.path is passed directly to subprocess.check_output with shell=True in the do_BDR() method. The entire path is executed as a shell command, allowing arbitrary OS command execution.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: SSL security features are explicitly disabled: SSLv3 is re-enabled (line 37), compression is disabled which reduces attack surface but indicates misconfiguration (line 38), and cipher suite preference is disabled (line 39). This weakens the TLS security posture.

Exception details are caught and converted to string, then returned to the client in the HTTP response. This exposes sensitive information about the application's internal state and file paths to potential attackers.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: User input from handler.params['path'] in SessionFixation.run() is injected into a JavaScript string in window.location without escaping. An attacker can break out of the string context to inject arbitrary JavaScript code.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
